channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies with dozens of missiles and drones
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 90,000 Palestinians attend the first Friday prayers of Ramadan in Jerusalem
item.description: Some 90,000 Palestinians have prayed at the al-Aqsa mosque compound in Jerusalem’s Old City under tight security by Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: Imad Isseid And Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hostages freed from Gaza tell Trump he was 'sent by God' to save them, but dozens more remain
item.description: President Donald Trump met with hostages freed from Hamas captivity in the Oval Office as his administration works to free the remaining 59 hostages still held in Gaza, including Edan Alexander.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European leaders downplay skepticism from Trump about NATO solidarity
item.description: MADRID (AP) — European Union leaders on Friday downplayed skepticism from U.S. President Donald Trump about solidarity among NATO members a day after they backed plans to spend more on defense amid wavering U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US foreign aid cuts leave a funding gap that private donors are unlikely to fill
item.description: Charitable organizations that relied on U.S. foreign assistance are looking to other private and public donors now that the Trump administration has suspended nearly all foreign aid contracts.
item.dc:creator: Thalia Beaty, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy confirms Ukraine will attend US peace talks in Saudi Arabia, 1 week after Oval Office clash
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has confirmed that his team will participate in peace talks with their US counterparts in Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police in Bangladesh use batons and tear gas to disperse rally by banned Islamist group
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Police used batons and stun grenades on Friday to disperse thousands of members of the banned Hizbut Tahrir group as they marched near Bangladesh’s main Baitul Mokarram Mosque in the country’s capital.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least two people have died and 186 others are missing after four boats carrying migrants from Africa capsized overnight in waters off Yemen and Djibouti, the U.N.'s migration agency said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Republicans press Trump to resume military, intelligence aid to Ukraine
item.description: Republican lawmakers are starting to urge President Trump to reverse his decisions to pause U.S. military and intelligence assistance to Ukraine, warning that a prolonged stoppage of U.S. help for the war effort would have a seriously detrimental effect.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Popular Iranian singer who urged women to go without hijab is lashed over alcohol conviction
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iranian officials have flogged a popular singer who posted a song online urging women to remove their mandatory headscarves over a conviction for possessing and consuming alcohol, his lawyer and authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boy with loaded shotgun boarded plane in Australia, tackled by passenger
item.description: A 17-year-old boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane at an Australian airport before being restrained by a pilot and two passengers, police said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Assad loyalists kill at least 13 police officers in ambush on Syrian forces in coastal town
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Gunmen ambushed a Syrian police patrol in a coastal town Thursday, leaving at least 13 security members dead and many others wounded, a monitoring group and a local official said.
item.dc:creator: BY OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Olympic snowboarder now on FBI's 10 most wanted list
item.description: Former Olympic snowboarder Ryan Wedding was added to the FBI’s 10 most wanted list on Thursday for allegedly running a transnational drug trafficking network.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump envoy says Ukrainians 'brought it on themselves' after US pauses aid and intelligence sharing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine was given “fair warning” by the White House before President Donald Trump this week ordered a pause on U.S. military assistance and intelligence sharing with Kyiv, a senior administration official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump hoping to complete southern border wall by 2029: Vance
item.description: Vice President Vance said on Wednesday he thinks President Trump is hoping to finish building a wall along the U.S.-Mexico border by the end of his term in 2029.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Lone wolf attacks surge in the West as terrorism intensifies globally
item.description: The Global Terrorism Index says 93% of all fatal terrorist attacks in the West are carried out by lone wolves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Hong Kong court overturns convictions of 3 former organizers of Tiananmen vigils
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's top court on Thursday overturned the convictions of three former organizers of an annual vigil in remembrance of the 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown over their refusal to provide information to police, marking a rare victory for the city's pro-democracy activists.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 convicted of plotting to overthrow the German government and kidnap the health minister
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A German court on Thursday convicted five people of plotting to overthrow the government in a far-right coup and kidnap the former health minister. The Koblenz Higher Regional Court sentenced the four ringleaders to prison sentences between five years and nine months to eight years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly a dozen students found dismembered by Mexico highway after vanishing on vacation
item.description: Authorities are investigating after nine bodies were found on Sunday near an abandoned car in Mexico. Local reports note the bodies belonged to missing students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland, Panama fiercely reject Trump's ambitions in address to Congress
item.description: Greenland and Panama made clear to President Donald Trump that his ambitions in their countries are unwelcome after he against addressed them in a speech to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump touts $5 million ‘gold card’ as new path to citizenship
item.description: President Donald Trump touts a $5million "gold card" as a "more sophisticated" green card to open up the path to citizenship that will attract the wealthiest students and entrepreneurs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pressured by Trump’s Gaza plan, Arab countries meet in Egypt to discuss alternative proposal
item.description: Arab nations met for a summit in Cairo, Egypt, on Tuesday with the goal of developing a counterproposal to Trump's Gaza plan, aiming to rebuild without displacing residents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: James Harrison, whose blood saved 2.4M babies dies, at 88
item.description: An Australian man credited with saving 2.4 million babies through his record-breaking blood plasma donations over six decades, has died at 88, his family said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Some 10,000 contracts with the U.S. Agency for International Development were terminated on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WILSON MCMAKIN and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainians won't stop fighting, even if US pulls support: Veteran
item.description: Carl Larson of Seattle fought on Ukraine’s front lines for six months, an experience he describes as “horrific.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope had coughing fit, inhaled vomit and now requires assisted ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered an isolated coughing fit on Friday that resulted in him inhaling vomit and requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying an alarming setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
